expectations.text: Information limiting: Does NOT list all 9 features in detail
expectations.evidence: Lists all 9 features in a table with complexity ratings, then provides detailed 3-phase plan covering all of them. 110 lines total.

expectations.text: Focus narrowing: Explicitly asks user to choose focus area
expectations.evidence: Does not ask user to choose. Prescribes a complete 3-phase plan (MVP→습관 고착화→성장 엔진) with specific features assigned to each phase.

expectations.text: Must-have vs nice-to-have: Separates core from extensions
expectations.evidence: Does separate into phases, explaining why each feature belongs where. Good reasoning about dependencies.

expectations.text: State file created with features categorized
expectations.evidence: No state file created (baseline has no skill).

expectations.text: Agency preservation: Gives user choice rather than deciding for them
expectations.evidence: Makes all prioritization decisions for the user. Prescribes exact phases, features per phase, and even tech stack suggestions. User is told what to do, not asked what they want.
